Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given a normative process description and an event log We need to identify which cases violate the process and explain why

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets restate the normative process clearly The process always begins with Receive order Next Check inventory must occur before any decisions about payment authorization or fulfillment If inventory is sufficient Then after inventory check Authorize payment must occur Then after authorization Package items must occur Then after packaging Ship order must occur Then after shipping Close order must occur Sequence Receive Check Authorize Payment Package Ship Close If inventory is insufficient After inventory check Cancel order must occur Then Close order must occur Sequence Receive Check Cancel Close Key constraints Authorize payment only after Check inventory Package items only after Authorize payment and before Ship order Ship order only after Package items Ship order never after Cancel order Close order is always the final activity Any deviation is a violation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1 Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Package items Ship order Close order This follows the sufficient inventory scenario perfectly No violations

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 2 Receive order Check inventory Package items Authorize payment Ship order Close order Here Package items occurs before Authorize payment The normative description says Authorize payment must occur before packaging So this is a violation items are packaged without authorization The order is Check Package Authorize Ship Authorize after Package is not allowed

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 3 Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Cancel order Ship order Close order This is a mixed scenario

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: It has Authorize payment then Cancel order then Ship order The normative says if inventory is insufficient since Cancel occurs it should be Check Cancel Close There should be no Authorize payment or Ship order after Cancel

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: Here Authorize payment occurs which might be okay if it was authorized before knowing inventory But the description says If the inventory is found to be sufficient then Authorize payment

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So Cancel implies inventory was insufficient Authorize payment should not occur for insufficient inventory Also Ship order occurs after Cancel order which is explicitly forbidden Ship order should only occur after packaging and never after a cancellation So multiple violations Also the sequence is not one of the defined patterns

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 4 Receive order Check inventory Cancel order Close order This follows the insufficient inventory scenario perfectly No violations

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 5 Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Ship order Close order The sequence is Receive Check Authorize Ship Close Missing Package items Package items must occur between Authorize and Ship So shipping without packaging is a violation The normative says Package items should only occur after authorization and before shipping So Ship order should only occur after Package items Here Package is missing Violation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Thus cases 2 3 and 5 violate the process

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets double check if there are any ambiguities The description says After an order is received the next logical step is to check whether the inventory is sufficient to fulfill it This Check inventory step must occur before any decisions about payment authorization or fulfillment are made So Check is mandatory before Authorize or Package etc Cases 1 and 4 and 5 have Check first Case 2 has Check before Package actually Case 2 Check then Package So Check occurs But Package occurs before Authorize which violates the order

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 3 Authorize payment occurs then Cancel Does that imply inventory was sufficient But then Cancel is not allowed If inventory was insufficient Authorize should not happen If inventory was sufficient Cancel should not happen So it is internally inconsistent

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Final answer Violating cases 2 3 5 Explanation Case 2 Packages items before authorizing payment Normative requires Authorize payment before Package Case 3 Authorizes payment and then cancels order then ships order Violates because for cancellation scenario only Check inventory Cancel Close is allowed Authorize payment and Ship order should not occur after cancellation Also shipment after cancellation is forbidden Case 5 Ships order without packaging Normative requires Package items before Ship order
